characters.name: Alicia
characters.description: AliciaCharacteristicsSpeciesKangarooGenderFemale ♀Personal StatusRelative(s)Wallace(adoptive brother)Friend(s)RockoHefferMediaFirst Appearance"S.W.A.K."Voice ActorLinda WallemAliciais a female kangaroo and mail carrier for the O-Town Post Office whom Rocko becomes attracted to in the episode, "S.W.A.K." Rocko makes many attempts to tell her that he has a crush on her but when he finally builds up the courage and presents a love letter to her, Alicia is revealed to be out sick for the day withHeffersubstituting her route. After Heffer mentions the possibility ofWallace, who is supposedly Alicia's boyfriend, not taking kindly to the love letter, Rocko attempts to retrieve his letter before it falls into the hands of Wallace. His attempts fail however and Heffer successfully delivers the letter to Wallace, who mistakes the letter as intended for Heffer. Despite this, Alicia apparently receives the letter beforehand and writes a response to Rocko, mentioning that she finds Heffer 'cute'. Rocko is heartbroken while Heffer becomes excited and leaves before Alicia arrives and reveals to Rocko that her letter was simply a prank to Heffer. It's implied that Alicia has a crush on Rocko.

characters.name: Ambulance Beavers
characters.description: Ambulance BeaversCharacteristicsSpeciesBeaverGenderMale ♂Professional StatusOccupation(s)ParamedicsPersonal StatusEnemy(s)Rocko Rama(sometimes)MediaFirst Appearance"Carnival Knowledge"Last AppearanceRocko's Modern Life: Static ClingVoice ActorTom KennyTheAmbulance Beaversare characters of a running gag throughoutRocko's Modern Life. They are two beaver paramedics who appear whenever a character is seriously injured. However, instead of taking the character who is hurt off on a stretcher, they take the inaminate object the character was injured on. An example is whenRockocrashes his bumper car at the carnival in "Carnival Knowledge" and the Ambulance Beavers take away the car instead of Rocko. When Rocko's appendix was hurting him in "Tickled Pinky," he fell off his jackhammer and they took the jackhammer away instead of Rocko. In the same episode, they tookHeffer, after they were told to (and they actually got it right that time), for emergency hippo-suction (liposuction) after he rushed to the hospital with Rocko in tow. Their catchphrase is saying "Hup!" repeatedly, while carrying a stretcher, loading the object on, and driving off to the hospital. When they carried Heffer, they said "Hup" slowly, since Heffer was heavy.

characters.name: American Balding Eagle
characters.description: American Balding EagleCharacteristicsSpeciesEagleGenderMale ♂Personal StatusFriend(s)Rocko Rama(currently)Enemy(s)Rocko Rama (originally)Filburt ShellbachMediaFirst Appearance"Rug Birds"TheAmerican Balding Eagleis a minor character ofRocko's Modern Life. He has an extremely large collection of wigs to conceal the fact that he is balding. This is wonderful for "bird-wig snatching#.Filburtwanted to steal the most valuable wig in his collection, and usedRockofor bait, as the Balding Eagle likes to eat wallabies, according to Filburt's bird book. However, Rocko convinced him to embrace his baldness, and he ended up throwing out all his wigs. He lives in a big birdhouse above a lake that looks a lot like a regular house. He is voiced byCarlos Alazraqui.

characters.name: Big Biff Benderhaus
characters.description: Big Biff BenderhausCharacteristicsSpeciesPigGenderMale ♂MediaFirst Appearance"Eyes Capades"Last Appearance"Zanzibar" (cameo) Big Biff Benderhausis a professional jackhammerer. He is also a terrible actor for commercials. Big Biff is a construction worker, jackhammerer, and actor. He is a great singer too. Big Biff wears contact lenses when he's working, but he wears glasses for anything else so he looks like a genius around women.

characters.name: Bloaty the Tick
characters.description: Bloaty the TickCharacteristicsSpeciesTickGenderMale ♂Personal StatusFriend(s)Squirmy the RingwormMr. IckkEnemy(s)SpunkyMediaFirst AppearanceDirty DogLast AppearanceRocko's Modern Life: Static ClingVoice ActorTom Kenny Mr. Ickk Bloaty the Tickis a parasite who live onSpunky,Rocko's pet dog. He has anOdd Couple-like relationship with his friend and fellow parasiteSquirmy the Ringworm. Bloaty's profession is not revealed, but he works for a grouchy bug boss namedMr. Ickk, who comes over for dinner one evening. When he's not at work, Bloaty likes to watch TV in his favorite "arm-scab." However, as seen in the episode "The Fatlands," he does lust for adventure now and then.

characters.name: Buddy Gecko
characters.description: Buddy GeckoCharacteristicsSpeciesGeckoGenderMale ♂Professional StatusOccupation(s)Lounge SingerPersonal StatusFriend(s)FilburtRockoMediaFirst AppearanceA Sucker for the Suck-O-MaticLast AppearanceRocko's Modern Life: Static ClingVoice ActorTom KennyLes Brown Jr.(singing voice)Buddy Geckois a famous American vocalist known as the "King of the Lounge Circuit" and a literallounge lizard. His name is a take-off of the real-life American singerBuddy Greco. Gecko and his music are greatly admired byFilburtwho secretly hopes to emulate him. His oeuvre includes at least one album:Loungin' Around. Buddy is shown to also have a side career starring in infomercials selling useless products for cash. These include theSuck-O-Maticbrand vacuum cleaners and the motivational tape sets "Geck-O-Netics".

characters.name: Building inspector
characters.description: Building inspectorCharacteristicsSpeciesSalamanderGenderMale ♂Personal StatusRelative(s)Ed Bighead(uncle)Bev Bighead(aunt)Rachel Bighead(cousin)MediaOnly Appearance"Keeping Up With the Bigheads"Voice ActorTom KennyTheunnamed building inspectoris a minor character ofRocko's Modern Life. He isEd Bighead'snephew, who is an employee of theO-Townmunicipal government, working to assess the state of properties in the city. His uncle Ed Bighead called him to condemnRocko'shouse in reprisal for a killer weed from his property destroying his prize begonias. However, he wound up condemning the Bighead residence instead after an mob of insects left it in an even worse state of repair than Rocko's.

characters.name: Captain Compost Heap
characters.description: Captain Compost HeapProfessional StatusOccupation(s)Compost HeapMediaFirst AppearanceZanzibarLast AppearanceRocko's Modern Life: Static ClingVoice ActorTom KennyCaptain Compost Heap(also called "CCH" by Heffer) was the compost heap and a character who made his first appearance in the musical-style episode "Zanzibar". He makes a brief appearance inRocko's Modern Life: Static Clingwhere he is seen singing the recycle song before he is crushed by Rocko's house after coming back to Earth, he is later confirmed to have died in a newscast.

characters.name: Claudette
characters.description: ClaudetteCharacteristicsSpeciesWallabyGenderFemale ♀Personal StatusFriend(s)Rocko RamaHeffer WolfeMediaOnly Appearance"I See London, I See France"Voice ActorLinda WallemClaudetteis a French female wallaby. She only appeared in the episode,I See London, I See France. A resident of Paris, Rocko fell in love with her at first sight when he saw her as he and Heffer were on a vacation trip to Paris. Rocko had very strong feelings for her, even calling her the, "girl of his dreams." Throughout the episode Rocko chased Claudette through Paris until finally catching up to her (andHeffer) at theChokey Chickenrestaurant in the Eiffel Tower. At the end of the episode, Heffer stated that Claudette probably really likes Rocko, but Rocko denied his feelings for her, stating that he likesMelba Toastmore.

characters.name: Conglom-O Lizards
characters.description: Conglom-O LizardsCharacteristicsSpeciesLizardGenderMale ♂Professional StatusOccupation(s)Conglom-OemployeesPersonal StatusFriend(s)Bev Bighead,Mr. DupetteEnemy(s)Rocko(Temporarily and Usually)MediaFirst Appearance"She's the ToadVoice ActorMaurice LaMarche TheConglom-O Lizardsare the employees that work atConglom-O. They work alongside the major characters ofEd BigheadandMr. Dupetteand are often scapegoated by the two. They make frequent appearances throughoutRocko's Modern Lifein episodes that feature Conglom-O. The lizards, like their boss Mr. Dupette are often depicted picking their noses. They are all voiced by Maurice LaMarche.

He's a walrus dressed in stereotypical sea captain garb, and has not only wooden peg leg and arms, but also woodeneyesthat are usually hidden under his eyepatches.

characters.name: Crazy Aunt Gretchen
characters.description: Crazy Aunt Gretchen. Crazy Aunt Gretchenwas Filburt's crazy aunt, who told him that if he ever touched one piece of candy, the Hopping Hessian would take him away. She appeared for a couple seconds in "The Big Answer", which was her only speaking role (voiced byLinda Wallem). Gretchen is considered crazy for never leaving her tank in her youth; resulting on her having to modify it when she got too big to move.

characters.name: Cupid
characters.description: CupidCharacteristicsSpeciesPigGenderMale ♂Personal StatusFriend(s)Ed BigheadBev BigheadEnemy(s)Ed Bighead(formerly)MediaFirst Appearance"Leap Frogs"Last AppearanceRocko's Modern LifeCupidis a minor character inRocko's Modern Life. He is the creature responsible for instigating love. He appears as a dark pink pig with wings which allow him to fly. He also has a magic wand and arrows that make people develop a liking for each other He appears in the episode "Leap Frogs", attempting to helpEdandBev Bighead'smarriage, which was in decline. He fails at this attempt though.

characters.name: Dan the Fly Man
characters.description: Dan the Fly ManCharacteristicsSpeciesAnthropomorphic DogGenderMale ♂Professional StatusOccupation(s)Fly delivery manPersonal StatusFriend(s)Bev BigheadSpunkyEd BigheadEnemy(s)EarlMediaFirst Appearance"Frog's Best Friend"Last Appearance"Ed Good, Rocko Bad"Dan the Fly Manhas three episode appearances. He only speaks in his first appearance, inFrog's Best Friend. Here he works for Maggot Farms fly delivery service (hence the "M" on his hat), and delivers a cage full of flies to the Bigheads' house and then gets attacked by Earl the dog who bites his hiney and also topples and wrecks his van. The last we see of poor Dan is whenMr. Bigheadis taking out the trash and seesEarlchewing on a tire; a sign reading "Help!" pops up from behind the overturned van. Dan hears something in the Bigheads' salmon bushes, and sees Earl's familiar punk rocker hairdo sliding along the top of them, like a shark fin, and runs for his van. He hears a growl behind him, and whirls to discover...Spunky! He playfully admonishes the little dog for frightening him...and is then promptly attacked by Earl. He later participates in two of the musical numbers in the episodeZanzibar!: "Spring Cleaning" and the reprise of "You Can't Fight City Hall" respectively. He is also one of the two characters who carry Rocko on their shoulders out of theConglom-Oboardroom at the end whenRockoconvincesMr. Dupetteto recycle. (In a coloring error, Dan is teal instead of purple in this episode.)

(In a coloring error, Dan is teal instead of purple in this episode.)

characters.name: Dingo
characters.description: DingoInformationNickname(s)Foofy No-NoCharacteristicsSpeciesWallabyGenderMale ♂Professional StatusOccupation(s)Student (formerly)Rocko's Bully (formerly)Pet of LingoMember of Hare KrishnaPersonal StatusFriend(s)Rocko(currently)SpunkyLingoEnemy(s)Rocko(formerly)HefferFilburtMediaOnly Appearance"Wimp on the Barby"Voice ActorCarlos Alazraqui DingowasRocko'sbully during his childhood in Australia. In the episode, "Wimp on the Barby", Rocko explains that he fled to America partly to escape from Dingo. Rocko met Dingo in the 3rd grade. and continued bullying him for 12 years. When Rocko's mom calls to tell him that he is coming to visit, Rocko panics, afraid Dingo will come to the United States and continue to bully him there. When Dingo arrives at Rocko's house, he reveals himself as a member of aHare Krishna-esque cult that opposes all types of violence and that he is now at peace with everyone, and is now known as "Foofy No-No". His ears are also gone when he visits Rocko. He presents Rocko with various gifts (a three-speed blender, Bacon-in-a-box, and $5) and asks Rocko to punch him in the nose for all the pain and suffering he put on Rocko for years. Rocko is apprehensive at first but does it anyway. As he leaves,HefferandFilburtambush him and attack him, unaware of his changed personality. He was voiced byCarlos Alazraqui. Despite his name, Dingo resembles a kangaroo more so than he does an actual dingo.

Rocko is apprehensive at first but does it anyway. As he leaves,HefferandFilburtambush him and attack him, unaware of his changed personality. He was voiced byCarlos Alazraqui. Despite his name, Dingo resembles a kangaroo more so than he does an actual dingo.

characters.name: Doctor Demento
characters.description: Doctor DementoCharacteristicsSpeciesRatProfessional StatusOccupation(s)DentistPersonal StatusEnemy(s)RockoMediaFirst AppearanceDental Hijinks Doctor Dementois a sadistic dentist that operates on Rocko's mouth. During the procedure to extract Rocko's rotten tooth, the machine explodes since Demento has a lit match nearby, causing both Rocko and Demento to be hospitalized. A letter to the editor printed in Issue #3 written by Joey Marchese of Union, New Jersey described Demento as "spoofed" by Mike Lackey and Rick Parker from the dentist in the first issue of Marvel Comics'Beavis and Butt-head. Mike Lackey responded, describing the similarities as "the strangest of coincidences, perhaps completely the work of planetary alignment or the magnetic resonance of the earth, or maybe the eclipse... Yeah! The eclipse!" He then continued, "ROCKO editor World Famous Mike Lackey looked directly into the sun while shuffling scripts, and...! Yeah, yeah, we don't buy it either!"

characters.name: Doctor Demento
characters.description: Doctor DementoCharacteristicsSpeciesRatProfessional StatusOccupation(s)DentistPersonal StatusEnemy(s)RockoMediaFirst AppearanceDental Hijinks Doctor Dementois a sadistic dentist that operates on Rocko's mouth. During the procedure to extract Rocko's rotten tooth, the machine explodes since Demento has a lit match nearby, causing both Rocko and Demento to be hospitalized. A letter to the editor printed in Issue #3 written by Joey Marchese of Union, New Jersey described Demento as "spoofed" by Mike Lackey and Rick Parker from the dentist in the first issue of Marvel Comics'Beavis and Butt-head. Mike Lackey responded, describing the similarities as "the strangest of coincidences, perhaps completely the work of planetary alignment or the magnetic resonance of the earth, or maybe the eclipse... Yeah! The eclipse!" He then continued, "ROCKO editor World Famous Mike Lackey looked directly into the sun while shuffling scripts, and...! Yeah, yeah, we don't buy it either!"
